message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Bar Cecil (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: French-leaning small plates / cocktail bar
Why it surfaced in discovery: Michelin Guide-recommended; widely cited as the toughest reservation in the Coachella Valley since opening in 2021; consistent best-of fixture (Time Out, Infatuation).
Discovery sources: https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/california/palm-springs/restaurant/bar-cecil | https://www.timeout.com/los-angeles/news/review-theres-nothing-in-l-a-quite-like-the-hardest-reservation-in-palm-springs-052725 | https://barcecil.com/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the web search results, here's what I found regarding Bar Cecil Palm Springs reviews:

## Review Counts

On Yelp, Bar Cecil has 547 reviews, and the restaurant has a 4.5-star rating on Yelp.

The search results didn't provide a specific Google review count, but the information available shows that Bar Cecil is a beautifully designed and impeccably managed Palm Springs restaurant and bar with countless rave reviews from critics, locals and guests alike.

## Review Highlights

Reviews consistently praise:
- Tasty dishes like ratatouille, chicken, deviled eggs, and standout bread rolls with date and sage butter
- Exceptional service with friendly staff who go above and beyond to accommodate guests
- Chic and contemporary atmosphere with very helpful and friendly staff

The restaurant is noted as being undoubtedly a popular spot, but securing a reservation is somewhat a challenge.

# Bar Cecil - Business Information

**Business Name:** Bar Cecil

**Address:** 1555 S Palm Canyon Dr., Suite H-104, Palm Springs, CA 92264 (Plaza del Sol)

**Hours:** Open Daily 5 pm – 10 pm (Kitchen closes at 9 pm)

**Contact:** 
- Phone: 442-332-3800
- Email: lovececil@barcecil.com
- Press: press@barcecil.com

**Menu Structure:** The establishment offers separate menus for cocktails, wine, spirits, and food service.

**Cuisine Type:** Fine dining with an emphasis on "beautifully crafted food and drink," inspired by aesthetic and artistic principles.

**Ownership:** 
- Lead partner: John Janulis (owner of Lightning Bar Collective in Portland and Villa Royale/Del Rey in Palm Springs)
- Majority owners: Richard Crisman & Jeff Brock (owners of Sparrows Lodge and Holiday House)
- Partner & Executive Chef: Gabriel Woo (former executive chef of Sparrow's Lodge and Holiday House; 2019 James Beard House guest chef)

**Key Staff:**
- Chef de Cuisine: Bryan
- General Manager: Jessica

**Awards/Recognition:** 
- Michelin recognition (2025)
- OpenTable Diners' Choice Badge (2024)

**Reservation System:** Online reservations available up to 14 days in advance; parties limited to 5 or fewer; 2-hour dining windows; walk-ins welcome at bar based on availability.

**Notable Policy:** Cashless establishment accepting major credit and debit cards.

Based on my search results, here are some of the best French restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area:

## Notable French Restaurants:

**Chez Pierre Bistro** - Located in Palm Desert, Chez Pierre Bistro offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list. Reservations are recommended as the wait time can be lengthy due to its popularity, and it's open from 5-9:30 p.m.

**Le Vallauris** - This restaurant, nestled in Palm Springs, offers an exquisite Mediterranean dining experience and has been enchanting guests since 1973, helmed by Executive Chef Jean-Paul Lair for over 25 years.

**Rooster Cafe** - This Palm Springs eatery brings the flavor of Southern France right to your table with locally sourced and made fresh daily menu items such as crepes, French press coffee, and Croque Madame for early-hour diners.

**French Rotisserie Cafe** - This establishment was listed among the top French restaurants in the Best of Coachella Valley 2025-2026 readers' picks.

There are also French & Belgian Bistros in Palm Springs serving French food and Belgian specialties, with a full bar featuring French and California wines.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil in Palm Springs:

## Bar Cecil - Palm Springs' Top Date Night Destination

Bar Cecil is The Infatuation's favorite place for date night in Palm Springs. The restaurant has gained significant acclaim and recognition in recent years.

### Reservations & Walk-in Tips

Reservations at this American bar/restaurant get nabbed weeks in advance—even during the low season—and if you think going midweek will help your chances, it won't. However, there's a helpful workaround: The bar area is walk-in only and that's where you'll want to sit anyway. The entire menu is offered here (get the pork chop), plus you can chat up the bartenders about the art on the walls while they pour you an Old Fashioned.

### Atmosphere & Vibe

Bar Cecil is one of those places that's sexy and chic without feeling like "sexy and chic" is the theme. It just naturally is—while also being a favorite place to grab dinner in town.

### Recognition

Bar Cecil is a MICHELIN restaurant, underscoring its high culinary standing.

### Adjacent Option: Beaton's

If you can't secure Bar Cecil reservations, Beaton's is Bar Cecil's adjacent lounge and a destination in its own right, best used for pre-dinner cocktails and bar snacks, in a room that looks like the VIP lounge at the Moulin Rouge.

Great! I found comprehensive information about the best restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026. Here are the search results from multiple sources:

## Highlighted Recommendations:

**From The Infatuation (March 2026):**

Rooster And The Pig is a family-run Vietnamese restaurant with excellent shareable dishes including tea leaf salad and turmeric cod. Bar Cecil is one of the tougher-to-book spots in town, with reservations getting nabbed weeks in advance.

Bar Issi, located in the Thompson Hotel on Palm Canyon, is an over-the-top Italian restaurant featuring bright pink hibiscus cosmos and puffy Neapolitan pizzas.

**From Time Out (January 2026):**

Many of the area's best restaurants are co-located within hotels, and during peak tourist season (winter and early spring), reservations are always a good idea.

**From OpenTable (May 2026):**

Clandestino serves elevated and fresh Mexican food located next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs, headed by Chef Gustavo Carreon.

**New Restaurants (2026):**

So-Cal Sunset, opened in 2026, boasts a large outdoor bar and patio with views of the San Jacinto Mountains and is perfect for romantic sunset dates.

Ash & Vine serves creative dishes with bold flavors, often fusing Asian and Italian fare, with recent highlights including bo kho rigatoni and birria de Borrego.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil, its owner John Janulis, and Sparrows Lodge:

## Bar Cecil

John Janulis is lead partner of Bar Cecil and owner of Lightning Bar Collective in Portland and Villa Royale / Del Rey in Palm Springs. Bar Cecil's culinary team is led by partner-Executive Chef Gabriel Woo, former executive chef of Sparrow's Lodge and Holiday House in Palm Springs.

The restaurant appears to have opened in 2021, as articles from that period discuss it as a new opening during the pandemic.

## Sparrows Lodge History

Originally built as Castle's Red Barn in 1951 by MGM actor Don Castle, it was one of the original resort getaways for Hollywood elite. Legend has it that iconic actress Elizabeth Montgomery had her first marriage at the Red Barn. The property also has had incarnations as Catalina Palms, El Rancho Lodge, and now Sparrows Lodge. In 2013, it underwent a full restoration, but its spirit remains the same.

Richard Crisman & Jeff Brock are majority owners of Sparrows Lodge and Holiday House.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil and its status as the hardest reservation in Palm Springs:

Since opening in 2021, Bar Cecil has been one of the most popular reservations in Palm Springs, if not the most difficult one in the greater Coachella Valley. Bar Cecil is the most sought after table in Palm Springs.

**Reservation Difficulty:**
Restaurant reservations are booked 14 days in advance and are difficult to get one or two of those coveted seats. Additionally, online reservations open at exactly 8 am PST 2 weeks in advance, so be ready to jump quickly. Getting a table at Bar Cecil is such an undertaking that even legendary LA Times food critic Bill Addison can't do it — he, too, happily snags a bar seat.

**The Signature Item:**
The most eye-catching item on the menu at Bar Cecil is the $50 Martini. Served on a silver tray, next to a deviled egg topped with a heaping portion of caviar and a side of sunchoke chips, this show-stealing drink ends up in front of most bar patrons and graces nearly every table in the 75-seat restaurant.

**Bar Seating Option:**
For those who can't secure a table reservation, the bar seating is reserved for first come first serve guests, and showing up right at 5pm will give you a great shot at scoring a bar seat.

toolUseResult: Error: Output does not match required schema: root: must have required property 'name', root: must have required property 'city', root: must have required property 'reputation', root: must have required property 'agent_visibility', root: must have required property 'visibility_gap_score', root: must have required property 'relationships', root: must have required property 'convertibility'

input.name: Bar Cecil
input.corridor: south Palm Canyon Drive (Plaza del Sol), Palm Springs
input.cuisine_or_type: French-leaning American brasserie / cocktail bar with small plates (oysters, steak tartare, pork chop, $50 martini with caviar)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 547 reviews; Google review count [UNVERIFIED] but Yelp + Tripadvisor + OpenTable + press footprint indicates a mid-to-large review band (estimated 1000-2000+ across platforms)
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive — 4.5 stars on Yelp; consistent praise for design, service, food (deviled eggs, pork chop, date-and-sage butter bread rolls), and bartender expertise; minor friction is reservation difficulty rather than the experience itself
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (2025-2026) continue to praise the room, service, and signature items; reservation difficulty remains the dominant narrative; bar walk-in seating is a celebrated workaround
reputation.longevity: Opened 2021 (approximately 5 years operating as of 2026); ownership has been stable — lead partner John Janulis with majority owners Richard Crisman and Jeff Brock of Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House; adjacent lounge Beaton's added as expansion
credentials: Michelin Guide recommended (inaugural California-Palm Springs coverage; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)
The Infatuation: named favorite date-night spot in Palm Springs; included in 25 Best Restaurants & Bars in Palm Springs
Time Out feature framing it as 'the hardest reservation in Palm Springs' (May 2025)
Palm Springs Life feature ('A Neighborhood Place')
InsideHook feature ('How Bar Cecil Became the Hottest Reservation in Palm Springs')

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best restaurants Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'hardest reservation Palm Springs', 'Michelin Palm Springs', 'best cocktail bar Palm Springs'. Surfaces moderately for: 'best restaurants Coachella Valley'. Does NOT cleanly surface for: 'French restaurant Palm Springs / Coachella Valley' — agents return Chez Pierre, Le Vallauris, French Rotisserie Cafe instead, because Bar Cecil is categorized as American/cocktail bar despite French-leaning menu. Does not surface for Palm Desert, Rancho Mirage, La Quinta, or Indio queries (correctly — it's PS-specific).
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate — agents describe it as a chic, art-filled brasserie / cocktail bar with hard reservations and a Michelin recommendation. Some sources mis-tag cuisine as 'American' vs 'French' (Yelp lists as Cocktail Bars; Michelin uses 'Brasserie / Contemporary'). The $50 martini-with-caviar signature and the Cecil Beaton design inspiration are well-captured.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. barcecil.com is built on Showit. No JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected. No visible OpenGraph metadata extracted. Hours, address, and phone are present as plain text only. Menus are served as static PDFs (Showit-hosted), which agents cannot parse as structured menu data. Site relies on OpenTable embed and Google Maps link for downstream structured signals.

competitors: Workshop Kitchen + Bar (uptown Palm Springs design district)
Bar Issi at Thompson Palm Springs (Italian, similar 'see-and-be-seen' tier)
Mr. Lyons Steakhouse (Palm Springs, similar cocktail-forward dinner vibe)
Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey (Palm Springs, comparable reservation-tight cocktail-driven destination)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — Bar Cecil is the dominant destination anchor for south Palm Canyon Drive / Plaza del Sol and one of the top 2-3 anchor references for the entire Palm Springs fine-dining scene; widely used as a benchmark in regional press

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — partnership group (John Janulis as lead partner with Richard Crisman & Jeff Brock as majority owners and Gabriel Woo as partner/executive chef). Related to but not owned by a hotel; Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House / Villa Royale share ownership overlap but Bar Cecil is a standalone restaurant.
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Strong. Custom Showit-built website with bespoke design and dedicated press email (press@barcecil.com), active Instagram (@barcecil, ~24K followers, 380+ posts), expansion into adjacent lounge Beaton's with its own site (beatonsatbarcecil.com), OpenTable paid listing, En Primeur Club listing, and sustained earned-media in WWD, Time Out, Infatuation, InsideHook, Palm Springs Life. The PR cadence and brand-extension strategy indicate deliberate marketing investment, even if no traditional agency footprint is visible.
convertibility.reasoning: Bar Cecil is a textbook convertible: independently owned by a reachable partnership group with proven willingness to invest in design, PR, brand extension (Beaton's), and digital presence. The press@barcecil.com address signals an existing comms/marketing point of contact. The primary visibility friction is technical — no schema.org markup, menus locked in PDFs, weak site-level structured data — which is precisely the kind of agent-readiness work this report is designed to surface. The reputation is well ahead of the on-site structured-data layer, so there is meaningful headroom for an agent-visibility engagement to add value without first having to rebuild the brand.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact Google review count — searches did not return a definitive figure; Yelp shows 547 reviews and a 4.5-star rating

[UNVERIFIED] Whether Michelin recognition is 'Michelin Plate' (recommended) vs 'Bib Gourmand' vs 'Star' — sources consistently describe it as Michelin Guide recommended / Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025, NOT a star or Bib Gourmand. Michelin's own page returned 403 on direct fetch.
[UNVERIFIED] Precise ownership equity split among Janulis / Crisman / Brock / Woo — described in interviews as a partnership but exact stakes not public
[UNVERIFIED] Whether the site has any non-rendered server-side structured data beyond what the WebFetch HTML capture revealed — assessment is based on visible HTML only

Cuisine label drift: described variously as 'French-leaning', 'brasserie', 'American with French accents', and 'cocktail bar' depending on source; the entity itself does not strongly self-claim 'French' on barcecil.com
